access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q27.explanation: Câu 27 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 8 chứa căn cứ: "Both processes run the risk of losing compounds that contribute to the appeal of the drink. For instance, caffeine sublimates (goes from a solid to a gaseous state without passing through an intermediate liquid state) at 180 degrees Celsius at atmospheric pressure. This high temperature in spray-drying chambers may affect instant coffee’s caffeine content and the content of organic compounds that provide taste and aroma.". Lựa chọn C (The statement refers to both the freeze-dry and the spray-dry coffee processes.) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q27.evidence: Both processes run the risk of losing compounds that contribute to the appeal of the drink. For instance, caffeine sublimates (goes from a solid to a gaseous state without passing through an intermediate liquid state) at 180 degrees Celsius at atmospheric pressure. This high temperature in spray-drying chambers may affect instant coffee’s caffeine content and the content of organic compounds that provide taste and aroma._

q28.explanation: Câu 28 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 5 chứa căn cứ: "The ground beans are then treated with hot water to recover the coffee flavour and aroma. This part of the process is similar to using a domestic coffee percolator. The coffee extract is then dried in one of two ways - freeze-drying or spray-drying.". Lựa chọn C (The statement refers to both the freeze-dry and the spray-dry coffee processes.) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q28.evidence: The ground beans are then treated with hot water to recover the coffee flavour and aroma. This part of the process is similar to using a domestic coffee percolator. The coffee extract is then dried in one of two ways - freeze-drying or spray-drying._

q29.explanation: Câu 29 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 9 chứa căn cứ: "Although the price of any retail instant coffee is determined largely by costs related to distribution, freeze-dried instant coffee production costs are 35 per cent higher than the costs of production by spray-drying. Another reason the freeze-drying process is more expensive is because making the same amount of instant coffee by freeze-drying methods requires larger quantities of better-quality beans than does the spray-drying process.". Lựa chọn D (The statement refers to neither the freeze-dry nor the spray-dry coffee process.) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q29.evidence: Although the price of any retail instant coffee is determined largely by costs related to distribution, freeze-dried instant coffee production costs are 35 per cent higher than the costs of production by spray-drying. Another reason the freeze-drying process is more expensive is because making the same amount of instant coffee by freeze-drying methods requires larger quantities of better-quality beans than does the spray-drying process._

q30.explanation: Câu 30 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 8 chứa căn cứ: "For instance, caffeine sublimates (goes from a solid to a gaseous state without passing through an intermediate liquid state) at 180 degrees Celsius at atmospheric pressure. This high temperature in spray-drying chambers may affect instant coffee’s caffeine content and the content of organic compounds that provide taste and aroma. Whichever process is used, the final product is usually packaged in low oxygen conditions, because the presence of oxygen will cause further loss of aroma and flavour.". Lựa chọn B (The statement refers to the spray-dry coffee process.) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q30.evidence: For instance, caffeine sublimates (goes from a solid to a gaseous state without passing through an intermediate liquid state) at 180 degrees Celsius at atmospheric pressure. This high temperature in spray-drying chambers may affect instant coffee’s caffeine content and the content of organic compounds that provide taste and aroma. Whichever process is used, the final product is usually packaged in low oxygen conditions, because the presence of oxygen will cause further loss of aroma and flavour._

q31.explanation: Câu 31 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 8 chứa căn cứ: "This high temperature in spray-drying chambers may affect instant coffee’s caffeine content and the content of organic compounds that provide taste and aroma. Whichever process is used, the final product is usually packaged in low oxygen conditions, because the presence of oxygen will cause further loss of aroma and flavour. This intensive processing means instant coffee contains little oxygen and has a water moisture content of only 1 - 4 per cent, much lower than that which microbes need to grow, allowing the product to last for more than 2 years without spoiling.". Lựa chọn C (The statement refers to both the freeze-dry and the spray-dry coffee processes.) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q31.evidence: This high temperature in spray-drying chambers may affect instant coffee’s caffeine content and the content of organic compounds that provide taste and aroma. Whichever process is used, the final product is usually packaged in low oxygen conditions, because the presence of oxygen will cause further loss of aroma and flavour. This intensive processing means instant coffee contains little oxygen and has a water moisture content of only 1 - 4 per cent, much lower than that which microbes need to grow, allowing the product to last for more than 2 years without spoiling._

q32.explanation: Câu 32 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 6 chứa căn cứ: "The remaining water freezes into ice crystals. A process called "sublimation" (similar to evaporation) is used to remove the ice, and dry grains of coffee are left. The quality of the aroma and flavour are protected by the low temperature and gentle drying conditions.". Lựa chọn A (The statement refers to the freeze-dry coffee process.) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q32.evidence: The remaining water freezes into ice crystals. A process called "sublimation" (similar to evaporation) is used to remove the ice, and dry grains of coffee are left. The quality of the aroma and flavour are protected by the low temperature and gentle drying conditions._
